problem: Why do 39.6% of Nutrimix first-time buyers not reorder?

hypotheses.hypothesis: Price friction: no reorder discount removes the trigger to act
hypotheses.evidence: Top lapse reason across runs: no_discount_available_this_time (7/81 lapsers explicitly cited)

sub_hypotheses.text: Rs 649 feels acceptable with a discount, not without
Initial purchase was discount-driven; full-price repeat feels like overpaying
Price-sensitive Tier 2/3 parents defer without a clear save trigger

hypotheses.hypothesis: Outcome uncertainty: no measurable benefit makes Rs 649 feel unjustified

sub_hypotheses.text: 5 weeks is too short to see visible nutritional change
Parents expect appetite/energy signals but see none in 30 days
Without pediatrician follow-up, there's no external validation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Social proof deficit: no community reorder signal leaves habitual parents without reinforcement
hypotheses.evidence: prior_peer_validation is top reorder DRIVER — its absence predicts lapse

sub_hypotheses.text: Parents who reorder are motivated by peer confirmation, not self-assessment
Lapsers lack WhatsApp/peer group reinforcement at the reorder moment

hypotheses.hypothesis: Competitive displacement: Horlicks/Complan reclaim attention at the reorder window
hypotheses.evidence: Horlicks retargeting ad at tick 32 was in stimulus schedule

sub_hypotheses.text: Established brands are recalled at 'time to reorder' moment
Without a counter-stimulus, default brand preference reasserts
